name: Kamala Harris
subject: I have only ever had one client: the people
body:
I’m reaching out today to ask you to make your very first contribution to
our campaign for president. I don’t mind if it’s $10, $5, or even $1: what
matters most to me is that you consider making a contribution of an amount
that is affordable and meaningful to you.

But before I ask, I am going to share a little bit about why our campaign
is doing things differently, and why every single grassroots contribution
-- no matter what amount -- means so much.

In the years since President Trump was elected, this administration has
aligned itself with white supremacists at home and cozied up to dictators
abroad, ripped babies from their mothers’ arms in a grotesque violation of
their human rights, and handed huge tax cuts to corporations and the
extremely wealthy while working families live paycheck to paycheck. They
have derailed our fight to stop climate change and save our planet,
sabotaged health care for millions of families, and imperiled a woman’s
right to control her own body -- all while using fear and lies to divide
and distract us.

We are better than this. But we’re going to have to prove it. We’re going
to have to fight for it. I’ve never backed down from a tough fight in my
life, and I’m not going to start now.

I’m counting on you to join me. If you believe in our vision for America,
I am calling on you to make your first contribution to our campaign today.
Whether it’s $1, $5, or $25, every single grassroots donation is another
vote of support for our movement.

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your
donation will go through immediately:

In the weeks since we launched our campaign, I have been traveling the
country to hear directly from voters about the pressing issues facing us
as a nation right now.

Every conversation at every stop on the campaign trail has inspired me.
It’s clear: people across our country care deeply about our nation’s
future, and they’re ready to get to work and fight for it.

We’re tired of the fear and division coming out of Washington. We’re tired
of leaders more interested in selling partisanship and protecting their
own pocketbooks than doing what’s right for the people.

That’s why from day 1, I made a commitment to do things differently. You
can count on me to speak truth, even when it’s uncomfortable. And you can
count on me to fight for you -- not the big corporations or lobbyists
calling the shots in Washington these days.

I am committed to building a movement of people with you -- a movement
where people in every community across this country are welcome to join us
and come together to reshape our democracy.

Ours is a campaign of, by, and for the people. That’s why every single
grassroots contribution matters. Will you make your first contribution to
our campaign today and join us in this fight?

name: Abigail Spanberger
subject: Proposed cuts to Medicare
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger For Congress
body:
You might have heard by now, but there are massive cuts being proposed to programs that seniors in our district rely on.
| | Peter,

I want to take a moment to talk to you about this administration's latest budget proposal. **You might have heard by now, but there are massive cuts being proposed to programs that seniors in our district rely on.** I have serious concerns about this, and I want to make sure I let you know why:

The Trump Administration's budget is proposing huge cuts that would completely change Medicare, Medicaid, and Social Security as we know it. It proposes the following:
1. _$845 billion_ in Medicare cuts over the next 10 years.
2. _$1.5 trillion_ in Medicaid cuts over the next 10 years.
3. _$25 billion_ in Social Security cuts over the next 10 years.

Those numbers are jaw-dropping enough when you consider how many seniors rely on these programs. But, as with all things, it's the details that matter most here, Peter.

The cuts to Medicaid would come mainly from eliminating Medicaid-expansion under the Affordable Care Act, which has helped millions of Americans and their families get access for the first time to health insurance -- including hundreds of thousands of Virginians who benefited from expansion in the Commonwealth last year. The cuts to Medicare come primarily from targeting so-called "wasteful spending." And, the cuts to Social Security would come in large part from reductions in critical disability insurance.

When I ran for Congress in 2018, I spoke to countless seniors who told me how important it was to them that we protect Social Security, Medicare, and Medicaid. These programs make up a lifeline for millions who would otherwise be struggling to make ends meet every day.

[**So today, I 'm asking you to sign my petition. Our campaign is collecting supporter signatures to protect our seniors by rejecting these massive cuts to Medicare, Medicaid, and Social Security. Will you please add your name today, Peter? >>**](https://act.myngp.com/el/R_-AasYJPrSiCPESiyT1WhlNsFYklFskKNb1IQYqmNU=/2wmsm_mIxrGkZYIEMcI0nBLn96-d_evaUDQ-I-sDMEg=) | [SIGN MY NAME »](https://act.myngp.com/el/R_-AasYJPrSiCPESiyT1WhlNsFYklFskKNb1IQYqmNU=/2wmsm_mIxrGkZYIEMcI0nBLn96-d_evaUDQ-I-sDMEg=)
---
I made a promise to those I spoke to on the campaign trail to protect and strengthen these programs. [**Please sign my petition if you 're with me >>**](https://act.myngp.com/el/R_-AasYJPrSiCPESiyT1WhlNsFYklFskKNb1IQYqmNU=/2wmsm_mIxrGkZYIEMcI0nBLn96-d_evaUDQ-I-sDMEg=)

Sincerely,

_Abigail_

Abigail Spanberger is running for re-election in a pure toss-up Central Virginia district.

**Our next fundraising deadline is March 31st, and everything we raise will be made public. So we need to make a strong showing, or Washington insiders will be more determined than ever to beat Abigail in 2020.**

[**Will you contribute a few dollars to help Abigail win? >>**](https://act.myngp.com/el/R_-AasYJPrSiCPESiyT1WhlNsFYklFskKNb1IQYqmNU=/xgTheGzEOgRMNa0YGMTxRLzni5yUPjdd4dxmV-vn11k=?refcode=em_pet_2019.03.20_b1_as_all&amount=25&amounts=10,25,50,100,250,1000,2800) | [CONTRIBUTE »](https://act.myngp.com/el/R_-AasYJPrSiCPESiyT1WhlNsFYklFskKNb1IQYqmNU=/xgTheGzEOgRMNa0YGMTxRLzni5yUPjdd4dxmV-vn11k=?refcode=em_pet_2019.03.20_b1_as_all&amount=25&amounts=10,25,50,100,250,1000,2800)

subject: President Trump Visits The Last Tank Plant In America, Which He Helped Save
body:
Today, President Donald J. Trump visits the Army’s sole surviving tank plant in Lima, Ohio. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2f3ebd6663&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **President Trump Visits The Last Tank Plant In America, Which He Helped Save**

Today, President Donald J. Trump visits the Army’s sole surviving tank plant in Lima, Ohio, “the first such presidential visit there since George W. Bush stopped by in 2003,” Loren Thompson writes in _Forbes_. “President Obama never visited, and the depressed level of military spending during his presidency nearly shut the place down. That is not an exaggeration,” he adds.

“President Trump understands the importance of the work that is done at Lima for the nation’s warfighters and for the workers in the local communities, which is why he didn’t make it to Davos this year but he will be in Lima.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=13700c855c&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “The Trump defense budget is helping to create good manufacturing jobs at good wages, including in communities like Lima that have fallen behind economically. The revitalized Lima plant will directly employ a little more than 1,000 employees,” White House Director of Trade and Manufacturing Policy Peter Navarro writes in _[The New York Times](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=dd7e478403&e=3ee4985eea)_. “The president’s bold decision to rescue the Lima plant from the Obama budget sequestration oblivion will reap this nation benefits for years to come.”
---
|
---
| Advisor to the President Ivanka Trump “is leading the administration’s latest bipartisan effort, this time to reform federal college education and job training, even for newly released prison inmates,” Paul Bedard reports for the _[Washington Examiner](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=39e87294f5&e=3ee4985eea)_. “With other bipartisan victories already under her belt, Trump this week turned to improving the Higher Education Act to help more poor students and bolster efforts to steer Americans to trades where the jobs are, including apprenticeships.”
---
|

name: BernieSanders.com
subject: Time is running out
body:
This won’t be easy.
# [](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/8915?t=1&akid=776%2E150750%2Edz0H4F)

Sisters and Brothers -
We may have been out-raised in our first 24 hours, but today we learned that we had more individual donations than anyone else. A lot more. Almost two times as many.
What Bernie says is true: they may have a lot of money, but we have the people.
Soon we’ll close the books on our first official FEC reporting deadline. After that we’ll share our numbers. But not just how much money we’ve raised, we’ll share how many donations we’ve received.
And what we want to do is post a number that makes it abundantly clear that we do indeed have the people, and that we are the grassroots campaign to beat Trump.
So we’re shooting for as many individual donations we can. The amount isn’t as important as the donation itself. We are just looking to rack up that number, but time is running out. So we have to ask:
[**Can you make a $3 donation to our campaign before our first, official FEC fundraising deadline ends? We have a chance to put up a big number of individual donations, and we’ll do it if you chip in using this link.**](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/10181?refcode=em190320-1-120&t=26&akid=776%2E150750%2Edz0H4F)
We are still toying with setting an audacious goal for individual donations before this deadline. How people respond to today’s email will let us know whether it’s possible or not. If you donate, we think we’ll be able to go for it.
All our best,
Team Bernie
* * *

With time running out before our first official FEC deadline of our campaign, we're aiming to reach as many individual donations as possible.

**Contribute $3 before the deadline to send an unmistakable message that ours is the grassroots campaign capable of beating Trump.**

subject: President Trump saves the last tank factory in the Western Hemisphere
body:
In 2012, the Obama Administration sought to close one of America’s premier defense facilities as part of mandated budget sequestration. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b5ca48cded&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • March 20, 2019
---
| | |
# **President Trump saves the last tank factory in the Western Hemisphere**

**In 2012, the Obama Administration** sought to close one of America’s premier defense facilities as part of mandated budget sequestration. Congressional Republicans managed to stop them, but the number of employees at the plant still plummeted to just 75.

“**Enter President Trump** ,” writes White House Director of Trade and Manufacturing Policy [Peter Navarro in _The New York Times_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f755220812&e=3ee4985eea), “with a far different view of the role of a strong military in both defending our homeland and revitalizing our manufacturing base.”

**Visiting the plant in Lima, Ohio** , this afternoon, President Trump told its workers what they mean to our country. “It is here in Lima, right here, that American greatness is forged with American hands, with American heart, and with American pride,” the President said. He also announced the addition of 400 new jobs at the manufacturing facility.

**America’s resurgence** over the past two years is captured neatly by two numbers. The first is economic growth, which just cleared 3 percent for the first time in more than a decade. The second is Gallup polling that reveals 6 in 10 Americans are optimistic that today’s children will live better lives than their parents—up from just 44 percent in 2011.

**The American dream is back** because President Trump understands what so many experts do not: that restoring American greatness is bigger than any one policy. While Washington spent years tinkering with more regulations and more bureaucracy, towns across our heartland withered. Regaining their trust meant renewing their faith in our shared destiny as citizens, including our economy, our military, and our people.

**Today, there are plenty of reasons** for that trust. Manufacturing jobs are being created at six times the pace of the previous administration’s final two years. Blue-collar workers are on track to receive almost $2,500 more in annual wages. Our military members have received their largest pay raise in nearly a decade, and more than $700 billion is being invested _this year alone_ to rebuild the strongest military on earth.

**“We don’t know** what challenges will come, what dangers we must face, or what opportunities we must seize,” President Trump told factory workers in Ohio today. “But we know that America will always be ready because of you.”

**[President Trump is making America secure by bringing back manufacturing.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a036aa4811&e=3ee4985eea)**

**Watch:**[It is right here where “American greatness is forged”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0710e2fc3d&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|

| [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a3f0a2862d&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **First Lady Melania Trump** hosted representatives from across the Federal Government at the White House this week, leading an extended discussion on how to help improve the lives of children across the United States.

**“Today** was a great opportunity to hear from these agencies on the programs that most align with [the Be Best initiative],” the First Lady said on Monday. “I am looking forward to working with the Interagency Working Group on Youth Programs to help children receive all the tools and support they need to be successful in life.”
